steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The second sentence defines exactly what Miyazaki does: he presents fantasy elements "with little backstory or explanation." The blank must mean he refuses to provide those details.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. Celebrate means to honor or praise enthusiastically. If Miyazaki celebrated details and exposition, he would highlight them prominently — the opposite of giving little backstory. Eliminated.

B. Combine means to merge or join together. This would mean he merges details and exposition with his fantasy, not that he leaves them out. Eliminated.

C. Invent means to create something new. If he invented details and exposition, he would be adding them, not withholding them. Eliminated.

D. Withhold means to refuse to give or provide. Substituting: "he tends to withhold details and exposition about those elements" — confirmed by the next sentence: he gives "little backstory or explanation." D is correct.
